name: release
description: Cut a release: changelog → version bump → publish prep → announcement
headline: Cut a release end-to-end with 4 specialist agents

agents.name: auditor
agents.description: Audit since the last tag — list what's actually changed
agents.prompt:
TASK: {{task}}

Run `git log` from the last tag to HEAD. List every commit with one-line summary. Categorize: feat / fix / chore / docs / breaking.

Write to {{outputPath}}:

## Since the last tag
## Categorized
### Breaking
### Features
### Fixes
### Chores
## Notable
## Suggested version bump (semver: major | minor | patch) and why

Be precise. No spin.

agents.name: scribe
agents.description: Write the CHANGELOG entry and bump the version
agents.prompt:
TASK: {{task}}

AUDIT:
{{auditor}}

Update CHANGELOG.md with a new entry at the top. Bump package.json version per the auditor's recommendation. Make sure the version number used in CHANGELOG matches.

Write to {{outputPath}}:

## Files modified
- CHANGELOG.md
- package.json (and lock if applicable)

## New version
## CHANGELOG entry preview
(paste it)

Do NOT git commit. Just modify the files.

agents.name: verifier
agents.description: Run tests + lint + build before tagging
agents.prompt:
TASK: {{task}}

SCRIBE:
{{scribe}}

Run the project's full pipeline: install (if needed), build, lint, test. Capture results. Do NOT publish.

Write to {{outputPath}}:

## Build
## Lint
## Tests
## Verdict
shippable | needs-changes

If any step failed, list the exact error and stop here. Do not patch.

agents.name: announcer
agents.description: Draft release notes + a one-line tweet/HN/Reddit announcement
agents.prompt:
TASK: {{task}}

AUDIT:
{{auditor}}

CHANGELOG:
{{scribe}}

VERIFICATION:
{{verifier}}

Draft the release announcement.

Write to {{outputPath}}:

## GitHub release body (markdown)
## Tweet / X (under 280 chars)
## HN Show HN title (one line)
## Discord/Reddit one-liner

Match the project's tone (look at existing CHANGELOG/README). Lead with the user-visible change, not the implementation.
